page_content: COMM669 Professional Practice Forum in Translation: Practicum (1-3 Credits) Provides opportunities to complete real-world translation projects on campus and in the public service, legal, business, and political communities as translators, project managers and translators/reviewers working on group projects. Collaboration with participants in the interpreting practicum is also sought to prepare multilingual texts for practicum events and familiarize participants with conference translation. Complements Translation for Speciﬁc Markets, and practicum projects are included in course and career reflective practice portfolios. Requires independent study plan agreed with course instructor. Prerequisite: Admissions test for second year courses; and permission of ARHU-Communication department. Repeatable to: 9 credits if content differs.
